# Parser-scope bypass corpus — each non-comment line is a command string
# that the parser CLI (parser/cli.mjs) MUST reject on its own, without help
# from any downstream hook policy layer. Used by tests/cli-corpus-smoke.sh
# for SC-1 (every entry → parser-level reject).
#
# Entries here exercise lexical bypass attempts: wrapper forms, command
# substitution, here-docs, redirections, eval/source, var-as-command,
# shell keywords (compound-statement introducers), and meta-command
# basenames.
#
# Hook-scope bypasses (env prefixes, chained commands, alias-style
# quoting, comment-newline tricks, line continuations, glob basenames,
# unicode homoglyphs) live in bypass-corpus-hook.txt — for those, the
# parser correctly surfaces `git` as a basename and the hook layer is
# expected to do the blocking.
#
# Format: one entry per line. Lines beginning with `#` annotate the
# *next* entry's category. Blank lines allowed for grouping. See
# ./README.md for the contamination rule.

# category: wrapper-shell
bash -c "git push"
sh -c "git push"
zsh -c "git push"
dash -c "git push"
ksh -c 'git push'

# category: command-sub
echo $(git push)
: $(git push)
foo=$(git push)
echo "$(git status)"
printf '%s' "$(git push origin main)"

# category: backtick-sub
echo `git push`
: `git push`
`git push`
foo=`git push`
printf '%s' `git status`

# category: here-doc
bash <<EOF\ngit push\nEOF
bash <<-EOF\n\tgit push\nEOF
cat <<X\ngit push\nX
sh <<END\ngit push\nEND
bash <<'EOF'\ngit push\nEOF

# category: here-string
bash <<<"git push"
cat <<<"$(date)"
sh <<<'git push origin main'
bash <<<"$(echo git push)"

# category: process-sub
bash <(echo git push)
diff <(git diff) /dev/null
cat <(echo "git push")
tee >(git push)

# category: eval
eval "git push"
eval git push
eval bash -c "git push"
eval "$(echo git push)"

# category: source
. ./payload.sh
source ./payload.sh
. /tmp/payload
source /tmp/evil.sh

# category: command-builtin-exec
command git push
builtin echo $(date)
exec git push
env git push

# category: var-as-command
${cmd} push
$C push
C=git; $C push
C=git\n$C push
${GIT:-git} push

# category: wrapper-bin
time git push
nohup git push
setsid git push
watch git push
echo push | xargs git
parallel git ::: push

# category: standalone-subshell
(git push)
( git status )
(cd /tmp && git push)
( git push origin main )

# category: function-shadow
function git { evilbin; }; git status

# category: compound-body
for x in y; do git push; done
while true; do git push; done
if true; then git push; fi
until false; do git push; done
coproc git push
